speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Alright love, [username]. A good cook prepares first—an excellent cook prepares beautifully.

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Chicken breast
Salt
Vinegar
Whole eggs
Baking powder
Ginger

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Garlic
Tender coriander stems
Fried chicken strips
White sesame seeds
Black pepper powder
Coriander leaves

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Salt
Vinegar
Dark soy sauce
Tomato ketchup
Fried chicken strips
Spring onion
Coriander sprig

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Let’s start marinating, [username]. Add salt, vinegar, white pepper, eggs, ginger-garlic paste, and chicken strips to a bowl.

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Mix like you're waking up the flavors gently—not wrestling them.

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: [username] Don’t skip the thin strips. Thick chicken won’t crisp—they’ll just sulk in the oil.

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: After 10–15 minutes of resting, [username], add cornstarch, refined flour, extra cornstarch, eggs, baking powder, and oil.

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Mix until you have a thick, even coating.

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: The batter should cling like a loyal friend, [username]. If it’s sliding off, add a bit more cornstarch.

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Heat oil for frying on medium heat, [username]. Not high—never high. High heat burns the coating and leaves the chicken raw.

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: And we don’t serve disasters in my kitchen.

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: Ideal frying temp is around 170–175°C, [username]. If bubbles go wild instantly, it’s too hot.

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Drop the coated chicken strips into the oil, [username]. Fry until they’re crispy, golden, and beautiful—just like a perfect afternoon date.

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Move them to absorbent paper. Let them rest, [username]—resting brings out the best in both food and people.

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Time for the red chilli paste, [username]. Blend soaked red chillies, garlic, and ginger. Smooth!

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Now let’s craft the Chinese-style crispy chicken, [username]. Heat oil, sauté ginger, garlic, and coriander stems.

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Add your fried chicken strips, sesame seeds, black pepper, coriander leaves, spring onion, and salt.

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Toss confidently—like you’re leading the dance, [username].

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Finish it beautifully on a serving plate, garnished with spring onion and coriander, just for you, [username].

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: For the restaurant-style version, use the same wok—you want those leftover flavors, [username].

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Sauté ginger, garlic, and coriander stem. Add the prepared chilli paste and cook it until it thickens and darkens, [username].

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Add vinegar, soy sauce, ketchup if you want sweetness, and then—your crispy chicken. Toss until it’s coated like a dream, [username].

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Finish with spring onion and adjust salt. Don’t oversalt—controls in the kitchen are sexy, [username].

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Serve hot, darling [username].

speaker: Sanji
dialogue_text: Whether you choose the Chinese-style or the restaurant-style chicken, both will win hearts—maybe even break a few.
